project.title: Potential proteins interated with CHRONO in human U2OS cells
project.projectDescription: We screened the protein partners that associate with CHRONO using immunoprecipitation followed with mass spectrometry (IP-MS). IP-MS identified proteins involved in multiple protein-protein interaction networks that are functional in multiple pathways and cellular functions.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: 5Myc6His-CHRONO protein was stably expressed in U2OS cells, followed by immunoprecipitation with Protein G agarose beads which were incubated with anti-Myc specific antibody, and the samples were separated by SDS-PAGE.  After Coomassie brilliant blue staining, the gels were soaked in 50 µL of pure water for 30 minutes, then removed and cut (1-2mm particles). Then, 1 mL decolorizing solution (50mM NH4HCO3: acetonitrile = 1:1, V:V) was added, vortexed for 10 seconds, destained at 37°C for 30 minutes, briefly centrifuged and dried; repeated several times until the blue faded. Then 500 µL of acetonitrile was added to dehydrate the gel until it was completely white, the liquid was aspirated and the tube was opened to air dry, 10 mM DTT was added until the liquid covered the gel, then the gel was incubated at 56°C for 1 hour, after cooling to room temperature, the liquid was aspirated, 55 mM IAM was added rapidly until the liquid covered the gel, and the gel was left at room temperature in a dark room for 45 minutes. The liquid was then absorbed, washed twice with 500 μL destaining solution and once with pure water, 500 μL of acetonitrile was added, vortexed for 5 minutes and finally air dried thoroughly. The gel was then completely covered with the 0.01 µg/µL trypsin and incubate overnight at 37°C. The next day, 5x volume of 50% ACN was added, vortexed for 5 minutes, centrifuged at 5,000 g for 1 minute, the supernatant was collected and repeated, finally the resulting supernatant was centrifuged at 25,000 g for 5 minutes, and the supernatant was collected for lyophilization. Peptides were analyzed by LC-MS/MS using an EASY-nLC1000 system (Thermo Fisher Scientific, USA) and a Q_Exactive plus mass spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific, USA)
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Thermo Proteome Discoverer 2.2 (Thermo Fisher Scientific, USA) was used for protein identification using the uniprot-homo database with the following parameters: Precursor ion mass tolerance: ± 10 ppm; Fragment ion mass tolerance: ± 0.02 Da; Missed cleavage site: 2; False discovery rate（FDR）of all peptide and protein identi¬fications <1%; Max missed cleavages: 2; minimum peptide length: 6; maximum peptide length: 144.
